sections.paragraph:
προδηλόω 1 aor. προεδήλωσα; aor. ptc. n. pl. gen. προδηλωθέντων; pf. pass. ptc. προδεδηλωμένος (Thu. 6, 34, 7; ins, pap; 3 Macc 4:14; Philo, Decal. 45; 50) to make known beforehand, reveal τὶ someth. (Diod S 20, 37, 1; Plut., Pomp. 636 [32, 6]; Jos., Bell. 2, 629) τὴν θλῖψιν Hs 7:5. Pass. οἱ προδεδηλωμένοι πατέρες the fathers, whom we have previously mentioned (cp. IG XII/7, 239, 23; 3 Macc 4:14) 1 Cl 62:2.—DELG s.v. δῆλος. M-M s.v. πρόδηλος.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pl. = plural

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 867
